title: Justice Department Drops Investigation of Fed Chair Jerome Powell
summary:
Big news from Washington: The Justice Department has dropped its criminal investigation of Jerome Powell, the chair of the Federal Reserve. The Fed is the central bank of the U.S., and it makes decisions about interest rates that affect everyone's money.

The investigation was about cost overruns (when a project costs more than planned) in renovating two Fed buildings. The cost went from $1.9 billion to $2.5 billion. The Fed said it was because of unexpected things like lead, asbestos, and inflation.

A judge had already blocked the investigation, saying it was an improper attempt by the Trump administration to pressure the Fed into cutting interest rates. President Trump has been angry at Powell for not lowering rates enough.

The U.S. attorney, Jeanine Pirro, said she will instead ask the Fed's inspector general to look into the costs. The inspector general has already reviewed the project twice and found no wrongdoing.

This decision clears the way for the Senate to confirm Kevin Warsh as the next Fed chair. Powell's term ends next month. But Powell might stay on the Fed's board, which would give him a vote on interest rates. All this drama happens as the war with Iran is causing oil prices and inflation to spike.
why_it_matters: This news shows how politics and the economy mix. The Fed's decisions on interest rates affect how much it costs to borrow money for things like cars, houses, and college, which can impact your family's budget.

keywords.explanation: The part of the U.S. government that makes sure laws are followed and investigates crimes.
An official check to see if someone broke the law and should be charged.
The central bank of the United States that decides interest rates and controls money.
The extra money you pay when you borrow money, like for a loan or a credit card.
When a project ends up costing more money than was planned at the start.
A person inside a government agency who checks for waste, fraud, or mistakes.

background_read: The Federal Reserve is the U.S. central bank. It decides how much it costs to borrow money, which affects jobs and prices.
Jerome Powell is the chair of the Federal Reserve, appointed by President Trump in 2018 and later by President Biden.
Kevin Warsh used to work at the Fed and has been chosen by President Trump to become the new chair.
Article_Structure: WHO: Jerome Powell is the chair of the Federal Reserve; Jeanine Pirro is the U.S. attorney; Kevin Warsh is the new nominee.
WHAT: The Justice Department dropped its criminal investigation of Jerome Powell over building renovation costs.
WHERE: The investigation was in Washington, D.C., about Fed buildings.
WHY: Because a judge said the investigation was an improper attempt to pressure the Fed into cutting interest rates.

perspectives.description: They say the investigation was unfair and meant to pressure the Fed to lower interest rates, which would hurt the economy in the long run.
They believe the cost overruns should be fully investigated to find out if anyone broke the law, and dropping it lets the Fed off the hook.
